2603.25531.title: Synchronous Signal Temporal Logic for Decidable Verification of Cyber-Physical Systems
2603.25531.paper_summary: Many Cyber Physical System (CPS) work in a safety-critical environment, where correct execution, reliability and trustworthiness are essential. Signal Temporal Logic (STL) provides a formal framework for checking safety-critical CPS. However, static verification of STL is undecidable in general, except when we want to verify using run-time-based methods, which have limitations. We propose Synchronous Signal Temporal Logic (SSTL), a decidable fragment of STL, which admits static safety and liveness property verification. In SSTL, we assume that a signal is sampled at fixed discrete steps, called ticks, and then propose a hypothesis, called the Signal Invariance Hypothesis (SIH), which is inspired by a similar hypothesis for synchronous programs. We define the syntax and semantics of SSTL and show that SIH is a necessary and sufficient condition for equivalence between an STL formula and its SSTL counterpart. By translating SSTL to LTL_P (LTL defined over predicates), we enable decidable model checking using the SPIN model checker. We demonstrate the approach on a 33-node human heart model and other case studies.
2603.25531.paper_summary_zh: 許多網路物理系統（CPS）在安全關鍵環境中運作，其中正確執行、可靠性和可信度至關重要。信號時間邏輯（STL）提供了一個檢查安全關鍵CPS的正式框架。然而，STL的靜態驗證一般是不可判定的，除非我們想使用基於運行時的方法進行驗證，但這些方法存在限制。我們提出了同步信號時間邏輯（SSTL），這是STL的一個可判定片段，允許靜態安全性和活性屬性的驗證。在SSTL中，我們假設信號在固定的離散步驟（稱為ticks）中進行取樣，然後提出了一個假設，稱為信號不變性假設（SIH），該假設受到同步程序類似假設的啟發。我們定義了SSTL的語法和語義，並表明SIH是STL公式與其SSTL對應物之間等價的必要且充分條件。通過將SSTL轉換為LTL_P（在謂詞上定義的LTL），我們實現了使用SPIN模型檢查器的可判定模型檢查。我們在一個33節點的人類心臟模型和其他案例研究中展示了這種方法。
2603.25531.author: Partha Roop et.al.
2603.25531.authors: Partha Roop, Sobhan Chatterjee, Avinash Malik, Nathan Allen, Logan Kenwright
